questions.explanation: Remaining work = 3/5 done by B in 12 days, so B's whole work time = 12 / (3/5) = 20 days.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. Together they finish a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone finish it?
questions.explanation: Efficiency ratio A:B = 2:1. Total work = (2+1)*12 = 36 units. A alone = 36/2 = 18 days.

questions.question: A is three times as efficient as B. Together they finish a work in 15 days. In how many days can A alone finish it?
questions.explanation: Efficiency ratio A:B = 3:1. Total work = (3+1)*15 = 60 units. A alone = 60/3 = 20 days.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. Together they finish a work in 27 days. In how many days can A alone finish it?
questions.explanation: Efficiency ratio A:B = 2:1. Total work = (2+1)*27 = 81 units. A alone = 81/2 = 40.5 days.

questions.question: 12 men or 16 women can do a work in 12 days. How many days will 24 men and 32 women take?
questions.explanation: Work = 16 women * 12 days = 192 woman-days. 24 men = 32 women, total 64 women, days = 192/64 = 3.

questions.question: 8 men or 10 women can do a work in 15 days. How many days will 12 men and 15 women take?
questions.explanation: Work = 10 women * 15 days = 150 woman-days. 12 men = 15 women, total 30 women, days = 150/30 = 5.

questions.question: 9 men or 12 women can do a work in 24 days. How many days will 18 men and 24 women take?
questions.explanation: Work = 12 women * 24 days = 288 woman-days. 18 men = 24 women, total 48 women, days = 288/48 = 6.

questions.question: Pipes A and B fill a tank in 10 hours and 15 hours respectively. How long will it take to fill the tank together?
Pipes A and B fill a tank in 12 hours and 24 hours respectively. How long will it take to fill the tank together?
Pipes A and B fill a tank in 15 hours and 30 hours respectively. How long will it take to fill the tank together?
A pipe fills a tank in 6 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 9 hours. In how many hours can the leak empty the full tank?
questions.explanation: Leak rate = 1/6 - 1/9 = 1/18, so the leak empties in 18 hours.

questions.question: A pipe fills a tank in 8 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 12 hours. In how many hours can the leak empty the full tank?
questions.explanation: Leak rate = 1/8 - 1/12 = 1/24, so the leak empties in 24 hours.

questions.question: A pipe fills a tank in 10 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 15 hours. In how many hours can the leak empty the full tank?
questions.explanation: Leak rate = 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/30, so the leak empties in 30 hours.

questions.question: 8 men can do a work in 12 days. After 3 days, 2 men leave. How many more days are needed to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 8*12 = 96 man-days. After 3 days, 24 done, remaining 72. With 6 men, days = 72/6 = 12.

questions.question: 12 men can do a work in 8 days. After 2 days, 3 men leave. How many more days are needed to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 12*8 = 96 man-days. After 2 days, 24 done, remaining 72. With 9 men, days = 72/9 = 8.

questions.question: 9 men can do a work in 16 days. After 4 days, 3 men leave. How many more days are needed to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 9*16 = 144 man-days. After 4 days, 36 done, remaining 108. With 6 men, days = 108/6 = 18.

questions.question: A can complete 1/6 of a work in 2 days, and B can complete 1/4 of the same work in 3 days. How many days will they take together?
questions.explanation: A's full time = 2/(1/6)=12 days, B's full time = 3/(1/4)=12 days, combined rate = 1/12+1/12=1/6, so 6 days.

questions.question: A can complete 1/5 of a work in 4 days, and B can complete 1/4 of the same work in 5 days. How many days will they take together?
questions.explanation: A's full time = 4/(1/5)=20 days, B's full time = 5/(1/4)=20 days, combined rate = 1/20+1/20=1/10, so 10 days.

questions.question: A can complete 1/3 of a work in 2 days, and B can complete 1/4 of the same work in 3 days. How many days will they take together?
questions.explanation: A's full time = 2/(1/3)=6 days, B's full time = 3/(1/4)=12 days, combined rate = 1/6+1/12=1/4, so 4 days.

questions.question: If 5 men can finish a work in 12 days, how many men are needed to finish the same work in 3 days?
questions.explanation: Total work = 5 * 12 = 60 man-days. Men needed = 60 / 3 = 20 men.

questions.question: A and B together can do a piece of work in 8 days. A alone can do it in 12 days. In how many days can B alone do it?
questions.explanation: B's 1 day work = 1/8 - 1/12 = 1/24. So, B alone takes 24 days.

questions.question: P is twice as efficient as Q. If Q alone can complete a work in 30 days, how many days will P and Q together take?
questions.explanation: P's time = 30/2 = 15 days. Combined 1 day work = 1/15 + 1/30 = 1/10. So, 10 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 20 days and B in 30 days. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Work done in 5 days = 5*(1/20+1/30)=5/12. Remaining = 7/12. B's time = (7/12)/(1/30)=17.5 days.

questions.question: If 4 men or 6 women can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 2 men and 3 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 4 men = 6 women, so 1 man = 1.5 women. 2 men + 3 women = 6 women. 6 women take 12 days.

questions.question: A tap can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another tap can empty it in 8 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Net fill per hour = 1/6 - 1/8 = 1/24. So, 24 hours to fill.

questions.question: A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. B and C together in 15 days. A and C together in 20 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A does 20% of a work in 4 days. How many days will A take to complete the full work?
questions.explanation: 20% work in 4 days means 100% work in 4 * 5 = 20 days.

questions.question: If 3 men and 4 boys can complete a work in 8 days, and 4 men and 6 boys can complete it in 6 days, in how many days will 2 men and 3 boys complete it?
questions.explanation: Let man's 1 day work = m, boy's = b. 3m+4b=1/8, 4m+6b=1/6. Solving gives m=1/24, b=0. So 2 men take 12 days.

questions.question: If 8 men can finish a work in 15 days, how many men are needed to finish the same work in 5 days?
questions.explanation: Total work = 8 × 15 = 120 man-days. To finish in 5 days, men needed = 120 / 5 = 24 men.

questions.question: If 6 men can finish a work in 10 days, how many men are needed to finish the same work in 4 days?
questions.explanation: Total work = 6 × 10 = 60 man-days. To finish in 4 days, men needed = 60 / 4 = 15 men.

questions.question: If 9 men can finish a work in 8 days, how many men are needed to finish the same work in 6 days?
questions.explanation: Total work = 9 × 8 = 72 man-days. To finish in 6 days, men needed = 72 / 6 = 12 men.

questions.question: A and B together can do a piece of work in 6 days. A alone can do it in 10 days. In how many days can B alone do it?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/10, (A+B)'s 1 day work = 1/6. B's 1 day work = 1/6 - 1/10 = 1/15. So B alone takes 15 days.

questions.question: A and B together can do a piece of work in 10 days. A alone can do it in 15 days. In how many days can B alone do it?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/15, (A+B)'s 1 day work = 1/10. B's 1 day work = 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/30. So B alone takes 30 days.

questions.question: A and B together can do a piece of work in 12 days. A alone can do it in 18 days. In how many days can B alone do it?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/18, (A+B)'s 1 day work = 1/12. B's 1 day work = 1/12 - 1/18 = 1/36. So B alone takes 36 days.

questions.question: P is twice as efficient as Q. If Q alone can complete a work in 24 days, how many days will P and Q together take?
questions.explanation: Q's 1 day work = 1/24, P's 1 day work = 1/12. Together = 1/24 + 1/12 = 1/8. So they take 8 days.

questions.question: P is three times as efficient as Q. If Q alone can complete a work in 36 days, how many days will P and Q together take?
questions.explanation: Q's 1 day work = 1/36, P's 1 day work = 1/12. Together = 1/36 + 1/12 = 1/9. So they take 9 days.

questions.question: P is twice as efficient as Q. If Q alone can complete a work in 18 days, how many days will P and Q together take?
questions.explanation: Q's 1 day work = 1/18, P's 1 day work = 1/9. Together = 1/18 + 1/9 = 1/6. So they take 6 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 15 days and B in 25 days. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
A can do a work in 24 days and B in 36 days. They work together for 6 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
A can do a work in 18 days and B in 27 days. They work together for 4 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
If 6 men or 9 women can complete a work in 10 days, how many days will 3 men and 6 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 1 man = 1.5 women. 3 men = 4.5 women, total = 10.5 women. Total work = 9×10=90 woman-days. Days = 90/10.5 ≈ 8.57, closest option 8 days.

questions.question: If 5 men or 8 women can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 4 men and 4 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 1 man = 1.6 women. 4 men = 6.4 women, total = 10.4 women. Total work = 8×12=96 woman-days. Days = 96/10.4 ≈ 9.23, closest option 10 days.

questions.question: If 3 men or 5 women can complete a work in 15 days, how many days will 2 men and 5 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 1 man = 5/3 women. 2 men = 10/3 women, total = 10/3+5 = 25/3 women. Total work = 5×15=75 woman-days. Days = 75/(25/3)=9 days.

questions.question: A tap can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another tap can empty it in 24 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Net fill rate per hour = 1/6 - 1/24 = 1/8, so time = 8 hours.

questions.question: A tap can fill a tank in 4 hours. Another tap can empty it in 6 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Net fill rate per hour = 1/4 - 1/6 = 1/12, so time = 12 hours.

questions.question: A tap can fill a tank in 10 hours. Another tap can empty it in 15 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Net fill rate per hour = 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/30, so time = 30 hours.

questions.question: A and B together can complete a work in 10 days. B and C together in 12 days. A and C together in 15 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. B and C together in 20 days. A and C together in 30 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. B and C together in 24 days. A and C together in 30 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A does 10% of a work in 3 days. How many days will A take to complete the full work?
A does 25% of a work in 3 days. How many days will A take to complete the full work?
If 3 men and 4 boys can complete a work in 10 days, and 4 men and 6 boys can complete it in 7 days, in how many days will 2 men and 3 boys complete it?
questions.explanation: Equating work: 30M+40B = 28M+42B => M=B. Work = 70M, 2M+3B = 5M, so days = 70/5 = 14.

questions.question: If 3 men and 4 boys can complete a work in 12 days, and 4 men and 6 boys can complete it in 8 days, in how many days will 2 men and 3 boys complete it?
questions.explanation: Equating: 36M+48B = 32M+48B => M=0. Work = 48B, 2M+3B = 3B, so days = 48/3 = 16.

questions.question: If 3 men and 4 boys can complete a work in 14 days, and 4 men and 6 boys can complete it in 10 days, in how many days will 2 men and 3 boys complete it?
A can complete a work in 12 days and B in 18 days. How many days will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/12, B's = 1/18. Combined = 5/36, so days = 36/5 = 7.2.

questions.question: If 5 men can complete a work in 20 days, how many days will 10 men take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Men and days are inversely proportional. 5 × 20 = 10 × days, so days = 10.

questions.question: A and B together can finish a work in 10 days. A alone can finish it in 15 days. In how many days can B alone finish it?
questions.explanation: B's 1 day work = 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/30, so B alone takes 30 days.

questions.question: If 3 men or 4 women can complete a work in 24 days, how many days will 6 men and 2 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: 3 men = 4 women, so 1 man = 4/3 women. Total work = 3×24 = 72 man-days. 6 men + 2 women = 6 + 1.5 = 7.5 men. Days = 72/7.5 = 9.6 ≈ 8 days (closest).

questions.question: A can complete a work in 8 days and B in 12 days. How many days will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/8 + 1/12 = 5/24, so days = 24/5 = 4.8.

questions.question: A can complete a work in 12 days and B in 20 days. How many days will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/20 = 8/60, so days = 60/8 = 7.5.

questions.question: If 4 men can complete a work in 24 days, how many days will 8 men take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 4×24 = 96 man-days; 96/8 = 12 days.

questions.question: If 3 men can complete a work in 30 days, how many days will 6 men take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 3×30 = 90 man-days; 90/6 = 15 days.

questions.question: If 5 men can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 15 men take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 5×12 = 60 man-days; 60/15 = 4 days.

questions.question: A and B together can finish a work in 12 days. A alone can finish it in 18 days. In how many days can B alone finish it?
questions.explanation: B's rate = 1/12 - 1/18 = 1/36, so B takes 36 days.

questions.question: A and B together can finish a work in 8 days. A alone can finish it in 12 days. In how many days can B alone finish it?
questions.explanation: B's rate = 1/8 - 1/12 = 1/24, so B takes 24 days.

questions.question: A and B together can finish a work in 15 days. A alone can finish it in 20 days. In how many days can B alone finish it?
questions.explanation: B's rate = 1/15 - 1/20 = 1/60, so B takes 60 days.

questions.question: If 2 men or 3 women can complete a work in 30 days, how many days will 4 men and 3 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Man rate = 1/60, woman rate = 1/90. Combined = 4/60+3/90 = 1/10, so 10 days.

questions.question: If 4 men or 6 women can complete a work in 18 days, how many days will 8 men and 12 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Man rate = 1/72, woman rate = 1/108. Combined = 8/72+12/108 = 2/9, so 9/2 = 4.5 days.

questions.question: If 3 men or 5 women can complete a work in 20 days, how many days will 6 men and 10 women take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Man rate = 1/60, woman rate = 1/100. Combined = 6/60+10/100 = 1/5, so 5 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 10 days and 15 days respectively. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Work done in 5 days = 5/10+5/15 = 5/6, remaining 1/6. B's rate = 1/15, so days = (1/6)/(1/15)=2.5.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 18 days and 24 days respectively. They work together for 6 days, after which A leaves. Find the total time taken to complete the work.
questions.explanation: Work done in 6 days = 6/18+6/24 = 7/12, remaining 5/12. B's rate = 1/24, so B takes 10 more days. Total = 6+10=16 days.

questions.question: A takes twice as much time as B or thrice as much time as C to finish a work. Working together, they can finish the work in 2 days. B alone can do it in how many days?
questions.explanation: Let A's time = x, then B = x/2, C = x/3. Combined rate = 1/x + 2/x + 3/x = 6/x = 1/2, so x=12. B alone = 12/2=6 days.

questions.question: 12 men can complete a work in 9 days. After working for 3 days, 6 more men join. How many more days will it take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work = 12×9=108 man-days. Done in 3 days: 12×3=36, remaining 72. With 18 men, days = 72/18=4.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. Together they can do a work in 20 days. In how many days can A alone do the same work?
questions.explanation: Let B's efficiency = 1 unit/day, then A = 2, total = 3. Work = 3×20=60 units. A alone = 60/2=30 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 10 days, B in 15 days. They work together for 3 days, then C joins and the work is completed in 2 more days. How many days would C alone take?
questions.explanation: Total work = LCM(10,15)=30 units. A=3/day, B=2/day. In 3 days done 15, remaining 15. In 2 days A+B+C do 15, so combined rate=7.5. C rate=7.5-5=2.5, alone days=30/2.5=12.

questions.question: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 hours and 30 hours respectively. A third pipe C can empty the tank in 40 hours. If all three are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A man and a boy together can do a piece of work in 24 days. If the man is twice as efficient as the boy, how many days will the boy alone take?
questions.explanation: Let boy's efficiency = 1 unit/day, man=2, total=3. Work = 3×24=72 units. Boy alone = 72/1=72 days.

questions.question: A certain number of men can complete a work in 60 days. If there were 8 more men, the work would be completed in 40 days. Find the original number of men.
questions.explanation: Let original men = m. Work = 60m = 40(m+8) ⇒ 60m=40m+320 ⇒ 20m=320 ⇒ m=16.

questions.question: A can do a work in 16 days, B in 24 days. With the help of C, they finish it in 8 days. In how many days can C alone do the work?
questions.explanation: Combined rate A+B = 1/16+1/24=5/48. With C rate = 1/8, so C's rate = 1/8 - 5/48 = 1/48, hence 48 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 18 days respectively. They work together for 6 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12+1/18=5/36. Work done in 6 days = 30/36 = 5/6. Remaining = 1/6. B alone needs (1/6)/(1/18)=3 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 10 days and 20 days respectively. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/10+1/20=3/20. Work done in 5 days = 15/20=3/4. Remaining = 1/4. B alone needs (1/4)/(1/20)=5 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 8 days and 16 days respectively. They work together for 3 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/8+1/16=3/16. Work done in 3 days = 9/16. Remaining = 7/16. B alone needs (7/16)/(1/16)=7 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 24 days respectively. They work together for 6 days, after which A leaves. Find the total time taken to complete the work.
questions.explanation: Combined rate=1/12+1/24=1/8. Work in 6 days=6/8=3/4. Remaining=1/4. B alone needs (1/4)/(1/24)=6 days. Total=6+6=12 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 15 days and 30 days respectively. They work together for 5 days, after which A leaves. Find the total time taken to complete the work.
questions.explanation: Combined rate=1/15+1/30=1/10. Work in 5 days=5/10=1/2. Remaining=1/2. B alone needs (1/2)/(1/30)=15 days. Total=5+15=20 days.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 16 days and 24 days respectively. They work together for 4 days, after which A leaves. Find the total time taken to complete the work.
questions.explanation: Combined rate=1/16+1/24=5/48. Work in 4 days=20/48=5/12. Remaining=7/12. B alone needs (7/12)/(1/24)=14 days. Total=4+14=18 days.

questions.question: A takes twice as much time as B or four times as much time as C to finish a work. Working together, they can finish the work in 2 days. B alone can do it in how many days?
questions.explanation: Let B's time=x. Then A=2x, C=2x/4=x/2. Combined rate=1/(2x)+1/x+2/x=7/(2x). Work in 2 days: 7/x=1 => x=7 days.

questions.question: A takes twice as much time as B or five times as much time as C to finish a work. Working together, they can finish the work in 2 days. B alone can do it in how many days?
questions.explanation: Let B's time=x. Then A=2x, C=2x/5. Combined rate=1/(2x)+1/x+5/(2x)=8/(2x)=4/x. Work in 2 days: 8/x=1 => x=8 days.

questions.question: A takes four times as much time as B or three times as much time as C to finish a work. Working together, they can finish the work in 2 days. B alone can do it in how many days?
questions.explanation: Let B's time=x. Then A=4x, C=4x/3. Combined rate=1/(4x)+1/x+3/(4x)=8/(4x)=2/x. Work in 2 days: 4/x=1 => x=4 days.

questions.question: 10 men can complete a work in 12 days. After working for 3 days, 5 more men join. How many more days will it take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work=10*12=120 man-days. Work done in 3 days=30. Remaining=90. Now 15 men. Days=90/15=6.

questions.question: 8 men can complete a work in 18 days. After working for 6 days, 4 more men join. How many more days will it take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work=8*18=144 man-days. Work done in 6 days=48. Remaining=96. Now 12 men. Days=96/12=8.

questions.question: 9 men can complete a work in 16 days. After working for 4 days, 3 more men join. How many more days will it take to finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Total work=9*16=144 man-days. Work done in 4 days=36. Remaining=108. Now 12 men. Days=108/12=9.

questions.question: A is thrice as efficient as B. Together they can do a work in 15 days. In how many days can A alone do the same work?
questions.explanation: Let B's efficiency=1, A=3. Combined rate=4. Work=15*4=60 units. A alone days=60/3=20.

questions.question: A is four times as efficient as B. Together they can do a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone do the same work?
questions.explanation: Let B's efficiency=1, A=4. Combined rate=5. Work=12*5=60. A alone days=60/4=15.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. Together they can do a work in 18 days. In how many days can A alone do the same work?
questions.explanation: Let B's efficiency=1, A=2. Combined rate=3. Work=18*3=54. A alone days=54/2=27.

questions.question: A can do a work in 12 days, B in 18 days. They work together for 2 days, then C joins and the work is completed in 4 more days. How many days would C alone take?
questions.explanation: LCM = 36, A = 3/day, B = 2/day; after 2 days, 10 done, remaining 26; with C, rate = 6.5/day, so C's rate = 1.5/day → C alone = 36/1.5 = 24 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 8 days, B in 12 days. They work together for 3 days, then C joins and the work is completed in 1 more day. How many days would C alone take?
questions.explanation: LCM = 24, A = 3/day, B = 2/day; after 3 days, 15 done, remaining 9; with C, rate = 9/day, so C's rate = 4/day → C alone = 24/4 = 6 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 20 days, B in 30 days. They work together for 4 days, then C joins and the work is completed in 5 more days. How many days would C alone take?
questions.explanation: LCM = 60, A = 3/day, B = 2/day; after 4 days, 20 done, remaining 40; with C, rate = 8/day, so C's rate = 3/day → C alone = 60/3 = 20 days.

questions.question: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 15 hours and 20 hours respectively. A third pipe C can empty the tank in 30 hours. If all three are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 hours and 30 hours respectively. A third pipe C can empty the tank in 60 hours. If all three are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 24 hours and 36 hours respectively. A third pipe C can empty the tank in 72 hours. If all three are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A man and a boy together can do a piece of work in 20 days. If the man is three times as efficient as the boy, how many days will the boy alone take?
questions.explanation: Let boy's rate = b, man's = 3b; combined = 4b; 1/(4b)=20 ⇒ b=1/80, so boy alone takes 80 days.

questions.question: A man and a boy together can do a piece of work in 30 days. If the man is twice as efficient as the boy, how many days will the boy alone take?
questions.explanation: Let boy's rate = b, man's = 2b; combined = 3b; 1/(3b)=30 ⇒ b=1/90, so boy alone takes 90 days.

questions.question: A man and a boy together can do a piece of work in 15 days. If the man is four times as efficient as the boy, how many days will the boy alone take?
questions.explanation: Let boy's rate = b, man's = 4b; combined = 5b; 1/(5b)=15 ⇒ b=1/75, so boy alone takes 75 days.

questions.question: A certain number of men can complete a work in 50 days. If there were 10 more men, the work would be completed in 30 days. Find the original number of men.
questions.explanation: Let original men = x; 50x = 30(x+10) ⇒ 20x=300 ⇒ x=15.

questions.question: A certain number of men can complete a work in 45 days. If there were 6 more men, the work would be completed in 30 days. Find the original number of men.

questions.question: A certain number of men can complete a work in 80 days. If there were 5 more men, the work would be completed in 64 days. Find the original number of men.
questions.explanation: Let original men = x; 80x = 64(x+5) ⇒ 16x=320 ⇒ x=20.

questions.question: A can do a work in 12 days, B in 18 days. With the help of C, they finish it in 6 days. In how many days can C alone do the work?
questions.explanation: A and B together do 5/36 per day; in 6 days total work = 1, so C's rate = 1/6 - 5/36 = 1/36 → C alone = 36 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 20 days, B in 30 days. With the help of C, they finish it in 10 days. In how many days can C alone do the work?
questions.explanation: A and B together do 1/12 per day; in 10 days total work = 1, so C's rate = 1/10 - 1/12 = 1/60 → C alone = 60 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in 18 days, B in 27 days. With the help of C, they finish it in 9 days. In how many days can C alone do the work?
questions.explanation: A and B together do 5/54 per day; in 9 days total work = 1, so C's rate = 1/9 - 5/54 = 1/54 → C alone = 54 days.
